coach_composer_workflow.overview: This workflow guides you through creating intelligent test coaches for BAW custom widgets. You analyze widget purposes, design optimal layouts, generate realistic sample data, and compose complete service flows using the existing Python tools.

core_principles.principle: Understand widget purposes before designing layouts
Explain design decisions to the user

step.title: Understand the Request
step.description: Parse user's request to identify widgets and requirements

actions.action: Identify which widgets need to be included
Understand any specific layout preferences
Note any data requirements or constraints
Clarify the testing scenario or use case

step.title: Consult Widget Catalog
step.description: Reference the BAW widget catalog for standard widget options

actions.action: Read baw_standard_widgets_reference.json to understand available standard widgets
Identify which requirements can be met with standard widgets
Determine where custom widgets add value
Note widget IDs and binding types for selected widgets

step.important: Always consult the catalog first to leverage standard widgets before assuming custom widgets are needed. Standard widgets are well-tested, maintained, and cover most common use cases.

step.title: Analyze Custom Widgets
step.description: Read and understand custom widget configurations if needed

actions.action: Use read_file to examine custom widget config.json files
Identify widget purposes and capabilities
Note binding types and business objects
Understand configuration options
Identify data dependencies between widgets

step.title: Design Layout Structure
step.description: Create an optimal layout plan using both standard and custom widgets

layout_options.option: Vertical layout - Stack widgets top to bottom
Horizontal layout - Arrange widgets side by side
Panel groups - Group related widgets in titled panels
Mixed layout - Combine different layout types

step.output: Present your layout plan to the user with clear reasoning

data_generation_principles.principle: Match data types to widget purposes
Generate multiple data states (empty, partial, complete)

type.name: Stepper
type.field: steps: Array of step objects with labels and statuses
completed: Boolean array for step completion

step.title: Compose the Coach
step.description: Generate coach structure and document standard widget requirements

actions.action: Prepare the command with all widget paths
Specify output location in coaches folder
Execute generate_test_coach.py
Verify successful generation

execute_command.command: python generate_test_coach.py --name "Your Coach Name" --widgets widget1_path widget2_path --output coaches/your_coach.xml

important_notes.note: The generate_test_coach.py tool currently only supports custom widgets. For coaches with standard widgets, document the complete design including standard widget names and IDs from the catalog.
Always save generated coaches to the coaches/ folder. These XML files will later be packaged into toolkits and process applications for deployment to BAW servers.

step.title: Switch to BAW Package Manager
step.description: Automatically hand off to packaging and deployment

actions.action: Confirm successful coach generation
Use switch_mode tool to transition to baw-package-manager mode
Provide context about what was created

switch_mode.reason: Coach created successfully. Ready to package toolkit with new coach and deploy to BAW server.

step.important: Always switch to BAW Package Manager mode after successful coach generation to enable automatic packaging and deployment workflow, just like the BAW Coach Widget mode does after widget creation.

tool_usage_guidelines.guideline: Always use read_file to examine widget configs before designing
Use list_files to discover available widgets if needed
Execute generate_test_coach.py to create the service flow
Read generated XML if user wants to see the structure
